summary:
Claim: Glycolysis is the most rapid sustainable method for ATP synthesis during exercise.  

Evidence: - Creatine phosphate (CP) regenerates ATP nearly instantaneously (<10 seconds) but only serves as a short-term emergency reserve due to limited cellular stores (~1–2%). - Glycolysis begins producing ATP faster than aerobic pathways and maintains high output within tens of seconds, enabling sustained energy delivery during moderate-to-high-intensity activities.  

Answer: B – Glycolysis
